stadium.name: Dark Tree Field
stadium.description: Located in the Cold North of Mundos Ferox, Mellondor’s settlement of Dark Tree lacks the funds for a proper stadium, but nature has provided a rugged open field just outside the settlement. The field is completely exposed to the elements, meaning players must contend with freezing rain, sheets of ice, and the tree's own grasping roots, while spectators warm themselves with blankets and campfires around the perimeter.

stats.pregame_ritual: Attempting to communicate with birds.
Eating a dandelion, stem and all.
Mimicking the call of the regional 'Gloom-Lark' for courage.
Painting a crude, snarling face on their stomach.
Tying a small, hand-carved wooden bird to their gear.
Trying to teach a stray dog a complicated and useless new trick.
Attempting to communicate with birds.
Eating a piece of honeycomb to 'sweeten' their victory.
Chewing on tree bark.
Chewing on a splinter of wood from a past goal basket.
Balancing the Trollball on their head for a full minute.

stadium.name: The Guilder's Field
stadium.description: An opulent stadium sponsored by the powerful Church of Pecune. The stands feature luxurious, velvet-lined boxes for bankers, who are often more focused on making deals than the game.

stats.pregame_ritual: Washing their hands with dirt.
Eating a dandelion, stem and all.
Lighting a small piece of parchment on fire and inhaling the smoke.
Meticulously cleaning each stud on their boots with a special brush.
Eating a raw onion, layer by layer, without crying.
Getting a teammate to pour a bucket of cold water over their head.
A team-wide moment of absolute silence to 'gather their thoughts'.
Sharpening the edges of their blunted weapon (an exercise in futility).
Humming a tune they believe only they know.
Lighting a small piece of parchment on fire and inhaling the smoke.
Avoiding eye contact with anyone wearing the color green.

preGame:
Folks, gather 'round, grab your ale, and prepare yourselves for a spectacle of sheer, unadulterated, glorious violence here at The Guilder's Field! Tonight, nestled in the opulent heart of New Prosperity, we witness the clash of titans! On one side, the slick, calculating machinery of The New Prosperity Profits, sponsored by the very Church of Pecune! These gentlemen, draped in velvet and smelling faintly of high-stakes banking, are here to prove that money—and sheer, relentless tactical cunning—can overcome any challenge thrown their way! And facing them, brave souls from the frigid, rugged North, are the Dark Tree Demon Kids of Mellondor! Battling through the treacherous wilds of the Cold North, they arrive ready to prove that grit, honed by freezing rain and the grasping roots of ancient timber, is the truest measure of a warrior’s heart!

The atmosphere here is simply electric, folks! The bankers in their luxurious boxes are practically throwing gold coins at the field, the hired hecklers are warming up their voices, and the market fluctuations themselves seem to be betting heavily on the Profits to make a sweeping score! But look closely at the contenders! The Profits rely on the dazzling artistry of their playmakers, like Milman Galvan, who claims to hum a secret tune only she knows, a ritual of pure, unadulterated confidence! Meanwhile, the Demon Kids have their own unique brand of pagan fervor! We watched Stelmach Whitnell performing his delicate, improbable act of balance, holding the Trollball steady on his very head! Such dedication! Such beautiful, rustic devotion!

Oh, and don't forget the legendary hero worship! While all those pampered champions of the realm—like Sir Tanos, the Bold, or the ever-present, overly dramatic Morgwae—can strut about Osterra pontificating on the Everwar, tonight, the true champions of the field are these players! The New Prosperity Profits have the financial backing, but the Dark Tree Demon Kids have the sheer, stubborn spirit of the wild! Will the polished steel of the Guild of the Black Sky prevail against the primal fury of the Dark Tree? Tune in, true fans! This Tuesday night, it's Trollball madness, and Nok the Corrupter is ready to scream it from the rafters!
postGame:
And the final, glorious whistle has blown! What a magnificent, bruising, earth-shattering display of Trollball action we have witnessed here at The Guilder's Field! The New Prosperity Profits have absolutely dominated the contest, carving up the Dark Tree Demon Kids with the ruthless efficiency of a well-placed treasury axe! The final tally stands at four points to zero for the away team, and folks, that means four points of undeniable, glorious victory!

From the opening snap, the carnage was spectacular! The action kicked off with Gerianna Merwyn taking possession, immediately running headlong into the fray, only to be snagged by a monstrous grasping root! The Kids tried to answer back, but the Profits’ relentless advance was unstoppable! We saw Pulchi Benyamin slide across that treacherous icy field, narrowly missing a tackle on Jarid Tudela, all while the frigid rain lashed down like divine displeasure! The Profits kept pressing, while the Demon Kids fought valiantly against the elements and the sheer predatory nature of the opposition!

But the true crescendo came when the offense clicked into high gear! Aufmann Tedric, that magnificent Dane Axe wielder, found the gap and fired a thunderbolt from the home zone, scoring that first point! And then, oh, heavens above, Faina Hendrix—she blasted a two-point touchdown from center field! The Profits are simply too much! Despite the valiant efforts of the Demon Kids, who managed to keep the ball alive through the halftime intermission, the Profits’ scoring machine ran too hot to stop! We watched Boleslaw Heathcote drag a tired Jarid Tudela to the ground, while Milman Galvan continually evaded the clutches of the grasping roots, delivering the ball right where it needed to be! It was a testament to skill, might, and the sheer financial muscle of the Profits!

The Dark Tree Demon Kids showed admirable tenacity; they weathered the storm, even when Gladdie Farika managed to injure Gerianna Merwyn! Yet, even the stoic nature of the Mellondor locals could not withstand the onslaught! The New Prosperity Profits proved once again that while the heroes of the realm spend all their time posturing about virtue, it’s the bankers who know how to score! A triumph of commerce over cold-weather combat! The Profits take the win, and the crowds in their velvet boxes are absolutely roaring for their fortunes!
